news.summary: In New York, Daversa has promoted Kenny Denton to Partner, recognizing his expertise as a talent strategist for early stage companies. With numerous executive searches completed, Kenny has aided in scaling tech disruptors like OpenAI, Doppel, Scale AI, Decagon, Brex, Zip, and Poolside. Having worked on hundreds of searches, he is known for his effectiveness in finding catalytic GTM talent, making him a valuable asset to the firm.
news.headline: Kenny Denton promoted to Partner at Daversa, known for helping early-stage companies find talent; key player in tech industry.

news.summary: Wall Street's recent rally slowed down as investors grew concerned about the economy's strength following disappointing job numbers and a surprise shrinkage in the U.S. services sector. The S&P 500 remained flat at around 5,970, the Nasdaq 100 rose by 0.2%, and the Russell 2000 fell by 0.2%. The Institute for Supply Management's Services Purchasing Managers Index dropped to 49.9 in May from 51.6 in April, significantly below the forecasted 52%, signaling a contraction in the sector that contributes to 80% of the U.S. economic output. Additionally, the subindex tracking prices paid by service providers hit a record high, indicating rising input costs. In May, private employers added only 37,000 jobs, far below expectations. This data created uncertainty among investors, reflecting in the market's cautious behavior after two days of gains.
news.headline: Stocks stall on weak economic data with S&P 500 flat at 5,970, Nasdaq up 0.2%, and small caps down 0.2%.ISM Services PMI falls to 49.9 in May, below 50 indicating contraction, questioning demand and economy strength. Prices paid by service providers rise to their highest, implying rising input costs. Private employers added only 37,000 jobs in May.

news.summary: As Treasury yields approaching 5% and Moody's downgrades U.S. credit rating to AA, investor confidence in government bonds drops significantly. Concerns rise over increasing fiscal deficits, growing interest expenses, and perceived lack of action to reduce debt. Criticism focuses on President Donald Trump's tax plan, known as the "One, Big, Beautiful Bill," which could worsen the fiscal outlook. The Congressional Budget Office estimates the plan will reduce revenue by $3.67 trillion by 2034, while only cutting spending by $1.25 trillion. This results in a $2.4 trillion rise in the federal deficit over the next ten years. The U.S. deficit-to-GDP ratio is already at 6%-7%, levels usually seen during crises like wars or COVID-19. The growing deficit indicates a challenging economic situation.
news.headline: US Treasuries face pessimism as Treasury yields approach 5%, credit rating drops. Trump's tax plan may worsen outlook with $2.4 trillion deficit increase by 2034. Deficit-to-GDP ratio at 6%-7%.

news.summary: SSR Mining Inc. (SSRM) has announced a temporary suspension of operations at Seabee due to power interruptions caused by nearby forest fires. The fires, situated roughly 15 kilometers north of the mine, have led to this decision as a precautionary measure. As of now, the blaze does not present an immediate danger to the site and infrastructure. The company, listed on Nasdaq and TSX, is ensuring standard safety protocols are followed to safeguard all personnel and assets at the site.
news.headline: SSR Mining temporarily suspends operations at Seabee mine due to power interruptions from forest fires 15km away.

news.summary: A report titled "Display Market Industry Trends and Global Forecasts to 2035" has been recently released, now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com. The report covers various aspects such as product types, technology types, end users, resolutions, aspect ratios, touch technologies, brightness, viewing angles, power consumption, durability, and key geographical regions. The global display market is projected to expand significantly, with an estimated growth from USD 167.47 billion in 2025 to USD 261.38 billion by 2035, reflecting a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 4.13%.
news.headline: Global display market projected to grow from USD 167.47 billion in 2025 to USD 261.38 billion by 2035, at a CAGR of 4.13%.

news.summary: Stocks in the U.S. were mostly higher during midday trading, with the Nasdaq Composite increasing by over 50 points on Wednesday. The Dow was up by 0.09% to 42,558.84, the NASDAQ rose by 0.31% to 19,459.23, and the S&P 500 saw a 0.19% increase to 5,981.93. Communication services shares surged by 1.1%, while energy stocks dropped by 1.7% in trading. The ISM services PMI decreased to 49.9 in May from 51.6 in the previous month, falling short of the estimated 52. Yext, Inc. (NYSE: YEXT) shares spiked by 33% to $9.08 as the company raised its FY26 adjusted EPS guidance above expectations. K Wave Media Ltd. (NASDAQ: KWM) also experienced a significant increase, jumping by 115%. For more details, visit a financial news platform.
news.headline: Crude oil drops over 1%, ISM services PMI falls in May. Dow up 0.09% to 42,558.84, NASDAQ rose 0.31%, S&P 500 rose 0.19%.

news.summary: MongoDB, Inc. (NASDAQ: MDB) is set to announce its first-quarter financial results on June 4, following the closing bell. Analysts predict earnings of 66 cents per share, showing an increase from 51 cents per share in the same period last year. The company anticipates quarterly revenue to reach $527.48 million, compared to $450.56 million in the previous year. On March 5, MongoDB reported earnings of $1.28 per share, surpassing the analyst consensus of 66 cents. The company's shares closed at $193.75, marking a 0.4% increase on Tuesday. For more details and the latest analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga.
news.headline: Wall Street is anticipating MongoDB's Q1 earnings with analysts expecting earnings of 66 cents/share, up from 51 cents. Quarterly revenue predicted to be $527.48 million, up from $450.56 million last year. MongoDB's shares increased by 0.4% to $193.75 on Tuesday.

news.summary: The Maryland Stem Cell Research Commission has released Requests for Applications (RFAs) for the July 2025 funding cycle. The application deadline is set for July 9, 2025. Researchers and organizations interested in applying for funding must submit their applications before this date to be considered for the available grants. The Commission encourages all eligible candidates to review the guidelines and submit their proposals in a timely manner. For more information on the application process and requirements, visit the Commission's website or contact their office directly.
news.headline: Maryland Stem Cell Research Fund seeks applications for July 2025 cycle, deadline July 9, 2025.

news.summary: Coca-Cola Beverages Florida, LLC ("Coke Florida") in Tampa has appointed Shannon Smith as the new Senior Director of Community and Stakeholder Engagement. Smith, who has been with Coke Florida for nearly a decade, previously served as the Director of Sales for the Tampa market. In her new role, she will oversee community engagement initiatives and maintain relationships with key partners such as the Tampa Bay Buccaneers, Publix, and others. Smith's experience in leading sales activities across retail channels makes her well-suited for this position. This move highlights Coke Florida's commitment to strengthening ties with stakeholders and the community.
news.headline: Coke Florida appoints Shannon Smith as Senior Director for community engagement after ten years with the company.

news.summary: In anticipation of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day on June 15, AARP Pennsylvania is shedding light on a troubling issue. Financial exploitation, a commonly underreported form of elder abuse, poses a significant threat to seniors. As the day draws near, with Harrisburg as the focal point, it's crucial to address this issue. The organization stresses the importance of recognizing the prevalence of financial exploitation among older individuals. Through their efforts, AARP is working to combat this issue, encouraging vigilance and education. On June 15, a call to action is extended to raise awareness of this critical matter affecting many seniors.
news.headline: AARP Pennsylvania raises awareness about financial exploitation in elder abuse ahead of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day on June 15.

news.summary: A recent study revealed that approximately 30% of adults in the United States have experienced some form of cyberbullying, with young adults aged 18-29 being the most affected group. The research found that 65% of those targeted by cyberbullying were women, and the most common platforms for such incidents were social media and messaging apps. In addition, 40% of those who were cyberbullied reported experiencing symptoms of depression as a result. The study also highlighted the need for increased awareness and resources to address the growing issue of cyberbullying in the country.
news.headline: Bonds surge following poor data, boosting bets on Fed actions.  #finance #markets

news.summary: AUBURN HILLS, Michigan - On June 4, 2025, Chrysler marked its 100-year anniversary with a special event on Detroit's Belle Isle featuring a historical vehicle drive. The iconic cars showcased included the 1934 Chrysler Airflow, 1948 Town & Country, 1955 Chrysler 300, and 1963 Chrysler Turbine. As part of their ongoing celebrations, Chrysler highlighted the brand's rich automotive history and legacy.
news.headline: Chrysler marks 100 years with historical vehicle drive in Detroit highlighting iconic models.

news.summary: Sun Life, a top independent stop-loss provider for self-funded employers, has released its 13th-annual high-cost claims analysis on June 4, 2025, based in WELLESLEY, Mass. The report reveals a significant increase in healthcare expenses, with cancer, cardiovascular issues, and musculoskeletal conditions being the primary contributors. The analysis brings attention to the escalating medical costs, emphasizing the challenges faced by individuals and companies.
news.headline: Cancer remains top high-cost health claim, while orthopedic conditions rising; Sun Life report finds.

news.summary: In Austin, Texas, CrowdStrike (NASDAQ: CRWD) has been named a Customers’ Choice in the 2025 Gartner Peer Insights™ ‘Voice of the Customer’ for User Authentication report. CrowdStrike stands out with the highest number of 5-star ratings among all vendors reviewed. The company boasts an impressive 97% Willingness to Recommend rating, drawn from a total of 206 responses received by February 28, 2025. Highlighting the increasing threat landscape, CrowdStrike emphasizes the importance of protecting against attacks that utilize stolen credentials and social engineering tactics.
news.headline: CrowdStrike named as a Customers’ Choice in the 2025 Gartner Peer Insights 'Voice of the Customer' for User Authentication with most 5-star ratings and 97% Willingness to Recommend, based on 206 responses as of Feb 28, 2025.
